Q7 MCQ Not checked
General Aptitude • COMPUTER SCIENCE • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

How may numbers can be made with digits 0, 7, 8 which are greater than ‘0’ and less than a million?

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
Number of ways to form one digit number = 2
‘0’ cannot be treated as one digit number
Number of ways to form two digit number = 2 × 3 = 6
Number of ways to form three digit number = 2 × 3 × 3 = 18
Number of ways to form four digit number = 2 × 33 = 54
Number of ways to form five digit number = 2 × 34 = 162
Number of ways to foron six digit number = 2 × 35 = 486
Total number of ways = 2 + 6 + 18 + 54 + 162 + 486 = 728

Q9 MCQ Not checked
General Aptitude • COMPUTER SCIENCE • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Given below are four pairs of words. Identify the one which is different from others.

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
Murky as well as Dusky – both refer to something which is dark or cheerless – similar in meaning;
Inter means ‘to place’ and exhume means to dig out - opposite in meaning.
Effete means lacking in vigour whereas fructuous means productive or fertile – opposite in meaning.
Benediction is an utterance of good wishes while execration refers to denouncement i.e. both are
opposite in meaning.

Q13 MCQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science and artificial Intelligence • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
S1: Inserting a node at the end of a singly linked list takes θ(n) time if only the head pointer is maintained.
S2: Maintaining a tail pointer in a singly linked list can reduce the insertion time at the end to θ(1).
Which of the following is correct?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
• Insertion at the end of a singly linked list requires traversal when only the head pointer is available, resulting in linear time. So, S1 is correct.
• If a tail pointer is maintained, the last node is directly accessible, allowing insertion in constant time. So, S2 is correct.

Which schema design is the most efficient for reducing data redundancy and maintaining data integrity within dimensions?

Solution

(b)

data integrity. It organizes data into multiple related tables instead of storing repeated information.

In a data warehouse environment, which OLAP operation is used to navigate from less detailed data to more detailed data along a dimension hierarchy?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
Drill-down is an OLAP operation that navigates from less detailed to more detailed data along a
dimension hierarchy. It provides finer granularity of information compared to higher-level summaries

Q24 MCQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science and artificial Intelligence • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Let N distinct integers be stored in an unordered list
What is the minimum number of comparisons required to find any element that is neither the largest nor the smallest?

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
Take 3 elements:
Compare two → find largest and smallest among them (1 comparison).
Compare the extreme (largest or smallest) with the third → identify largest and smallest
(2nd comparison).
The remaining element is guaranteed neither largest nor smallest.
By using example:
List [1, 2, 3] → pick [1, 3] and compare (1 comparison) → we get 1 and 3, but 2 (middle element)
is missed.
Hence 1 comparison is not enough.
Answer is 2 comparisons.

Consider a relation P(a, b) which contain p tuples p > 0 and relation Q(b, c) contain q tupples (q > 0), makes no assumption about keys, what is the maximum and minimum number of tupples in the expression “P ∪ Q”?

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
P ∪ Q
Maximum = p + q if both relation is disjoint then maximum possible tuple is p + q.
Minimum = max(p, q) if one relation is result of another.

Q29 MSQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science and artificial Intelligence • +1 / -0
Not attempted

Which of the following statements is/are correct?

Correct answer: B, D
Solution

(b, d)
• This statement describes the exponential growth of nodes due to branching factor in a game
tree. It is unrelated to evaluation errors caused by depth-limited search. So, option (a) is
incorrect.
• The horizon effect occurs when an unfavorable outcome is delayed just beyond the search
cutoff depth. As a result, the algorithm incorrectly evaluates the current position as safe. So,
option (b) is correct.
• The horizon effect is not specific to alpha-beta pruning. It occurs even in basic minimax search
and is independent of pruning capability. So, option (c) is incorrect.
• The horizon effect is a known limitation of fixed-depth search algorithms, as they cannot
account for consequences that lie beyond the depth limit. So, option (d) is correct.

Consider the following relation with given functional dependencies:
X(P, Q, R, S, T) FD’s = {P → QR, RS → T, Q → S, T → P}
If X is decomposed then decomposition of relation X into X1(PQR) and X2(PST) is

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
Relation X(P, Q, R, S, T)
X1(PQR) and X2(PST)
P is the common attribute and key for X1 so decomposition is lossless join.
But is not dependency preserving because Q → S, RS → T is not covered.

Q47 NAT Not checked
Technical • Data Science and artificial Intelligence • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Consider the directed graph given below:

 
Consider the following BFS traversal of the graph starting from vertex P.
I. PQRTMSNU
II. PQTNRMUS
III.PQRTNMSU
IV. PQRMSTNU
The number of valid BFS traversal are _________.
NAT
Correct answer: 1
Solution

1
Considering each traversal,
• PQTNRMUS : Not possible because it’s BFS so after vertex ‘Q’ vertex ‘R’ will be traversed.
• PQRTNMSU : After vertex ‘T’, a vertex which is adjacent to vertex ‘R’ should be traversed.
• PQRMSTNU : Since ‘Q’ is traversed before, R, hence vertex adjacent to ‘Q’ must be traversed
before vector adjacent to vertex ‘R’.

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)

reaching the node.

Q53 MSQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science and artificial Intelligence • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Which of the following statements is/are correct?

Correct answer: B, C
Solution

(b, c)
• LOOCV (Leave-One-Out Cross-Validation) can be applied to both regression and classification
problems. So, option (a) is incorrect.
• In LOOCV, each model is trained on n – 1 points, so the models are highly similar, which
causes high variance in the estimate. So, option (b) is correct.
• Techniques like PCA or LDA reduce feature space, remove noise, and improve model
performance, especially for high-dimensional datasets. So, option (c) is correct.
• Forward selection is a general feature selection method and can be applied to any supervised
learning model where a performance metric can be evaluated. So, option (d) is incorrect.

Q54 MSQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science and artificial Intelligence • +2 / -0
Not attempted

The following relations keep track of airline flight information:

 
 
Note that the Employees relation describes pilots and other kinds of employees as well: every pilot is certified for some aircraft, and only pilots are certified to fly.
Which of the following query correctly find the eids of employees who make the second highest salary?
Correct answer: C, D
Solution

(c, d)
The RA approach is to first find all the employes who do not have the highest salary. Remove
these from the original list of employees and what is left is the highest paid employees. Remove
the highest paid employees from the original list. What is left is the second highest paid employees
together with the rest of the employees. Then find the highest paid employees of this new list.
This is the list of the second highest paid employees.

Q55 MCQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science and artificial Intelligence • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Consider the following statements regarding linear regression:
S1 : Multicollinearity occurs when predictors are uncorrelated.
S2 : Normality of residuals is required for predicting new observations.
Which of the above statements is/are correct?

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
• Multicollinearity arises when two or more predictors are highly correlated, not uncorrelated.
High correlation among predictors can inflate variance of coefficient estimates. So, S1 is incorrect.
• Normality of residuals is assumed for inference purposes (e.g., t-tests, confidence intervals),
but it is not required for making predictions using the regression model. Predictions can still
be made if residuals are non-normal. So, S2 is incorrect.

Q56 MCQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science and artificial Intelligence • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
I. By using binary search instead of linear search insertion sort take Ο(n log n) time in worst case.
II. Bubble sort runs in Ο(n) time if input sequence in ascending order.
III. Heap sort runs in Ο(n log n) time if input sequence in descending order.
Which of the above statement(s) is/are true?

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
I. By using binary search insertion sort take Ο(n2) in worst case because number of swap not change.
II. Bubble sort runs in Ο(n) time in sorted order input.
III. Heap sort runs in Ο(n log n) time, input sequence is ascending or descending order.
